channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Public confidence in US health agencies declines, survey says
item.description: Trust in health institutions tended to fall along party lines, with Democrats showing greater support.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: West Virginia governor's order leads to nearly 60 suspected illegal immigrant criminals detained
item.description: An illegal immigrant crackdown in West Virginia is yielding dozens of detentions as of Tuesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What Trump's funding freeze could mean for universities, nonprofits and more
item.description: A White House directive to pause federal grants and loans has raised uncertainty for many universities, states and nonprofit organizations.
item.dc:creator: David A. Lieb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Head Start and Medicaid providers hit glitches as Trump freezes federal money
item.description: As the White House paused federal grants and loans in a far-reaching executive order, early childhood education centers and states discovered Tuesday they could no longer access money they rely on to provide care for some of the nation’s neediest families and children.
item.dc:creator: Moriah Balingit, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump issues order to ban transgender troops from serving openly in the military
item.description: The executive order speaks of transgender identity in sweeping and dismissive terms, and sets the stage for a policy that is more restrictive and punitive than the ban from Trump’s first term.
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin, MPR News

item.title: Minneapolis police chief calls Trump Jan. 6 pardons ‘slap in the face’ to officers
item.description: Chief Brian O'Hara has joined a group of law enforcement leaders decrying President Donald Trump’s pardons for all who rioted at the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021.
item.dc:creator: Emily Bright and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Democrats to hold ‘emergency’ meeting on Trump’s spending freeze
item.description: House Democratic leaders have arranged an “emergency” meeting of the full caucus on Wednesday in response to President Trump’s decision to freeze a large chunk of federal spending.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some Trump voters are skeptical of his opening moves to embrace fellow billionaires
item.description: MESA, Ariz. (AP) — Enrique Lopez votes sporadically but bought into Donald Trump’s vows to fight for everyday workers, helping the Republican flip Arizona last year. Then the home construction contractor watched how the billionaire president opened his second administration.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, BILL BARROW and AMELIA THOMSON DeVEAUX, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Donald Trump and Project 2025 previewed the federal grant freeze
item.description: A sweeping White House order to freeze federal grants and allocations reflects a theory of presidential power that Donald Trump clearly endorsed during his 2024 campaign.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Kash Patel’s podcast persona: Staunch Trump defender and fierce critic of the FBI he could soon lead
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI agents who searched Donald Trump’s Mar-a-Lago estate found boxes of classified documents in his office and storage room and retrieved sensitive government secrets about nuclear systems and weapons capabilities.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALI SWENSON and AARON KESSLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Speaker Johnson brushes aside economic concerns associated with ICE raids
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is brushing aside economic concerns associated with the raids that Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officials have carried out in recent days, as Democrats warn that the mass deportations could harm the economy.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Karoline Leavitt, the youngest White House press secretary, makes her debut in the briefing room
item.description: The youngest person to serve as White House press secretary has made her debut in the briefing room, promising to hold briefings often and saying President Donald Trump also will be available to reporters for questions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans
item.description: A federal judge on Tuesday temporarily blocked a push from President Donald Trump to pause federal funding while his administration conducts an across-the-board ideological review to uproot progressive initiatives.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs executive order taking aim at DEI programs in the military
item.description: Trump said the diversity programs discourage merit and leadership, and discriminate on the basis of race and sex. The directive was among a series of orders that seeks to reshape the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie, MPR News

item.title: Doctors opposing RFK Jr. rally in the lead-up to his confirmation
item.description: Many health professionals are lining up against Trump's pick for health secretary. They say his anti-vaccine views could cost lives. Some of his supporters embrace his stance.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, MPR News

item.title: Voters to receive postcards for District 1 special primary election
item.description: Registered voters in San Diego County's First Supervisorial District will soon receive postcards for the April 8 special primary election.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Trump-endorsed candidates win primary races to replace Matt Gaetz and Mike Waltz
item.description: President Donald Trump-endorsed candidates Jimmy Patronis and Randy Fine won their Republican primaries in the special elections to replace former Florida Reps. Matt Gaetz and Mike Waltz, notching wins in reliably conservative districts.
item.dc:creator: Kate Payne And Stephany Matat, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump Justice Department says it has fired employees involved in prosecutions of the president
item.description: The Justice Department said Monday that it had fired more than a dozen employees who worked on criminal prosecutions of President Donald Trump, moving rapidly to pursue retribution against lawyers involved in the investigations and signaling an early willingness to take action favorable to the presi
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Johnson opens door to linking voter ID to California disaster aid after Trump demand
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is keeping the door open to conditioning California disaster aid on the state enacting voter identification laws, days after President Trump said he wanted to link the two priorities.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Bishop who enraged Trump says she was speaking from faith
item.description: Trump, who sat alongside JD Vance, appeared annoyed, looking away from Budde as she spoke directly to him.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: New Trump orders on transgender troops, COVID and more expected on Hegseth’s first day
item.description: President Donald Trump says he will sign a flurry of executive orders focused on the military, including to reinstate troops booted for refusing COVID-19 vaccines and remove “transgender ideology” from the force.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump huddles with House GOP at retreat in Florida
item.description: GOP leaders will try to move Trump’s legislative agenda, including an extension of 2017 tax cuts and border policy.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: White House says Colombia will take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said Sunday night that a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth between the two countries over topics including immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Three of Trump's most controversial Cabinet picks set for hearings
item.description: More hearings have been scheduled for Trump’s Cabinet nominees, marking a significant week ahead for some of his more controversial picks.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard's path through Intelligence Committee narrows ahead of key hearing
item.description: Republicans are cautioning that Tulsi Gabbard’s path to confirmation to lead the U.S. intelligence apparatus is narrowing as she seemingly has trouble winning over key GOP senators. Gabbard, along with Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's move to freeze Biden-approved funding draws howls from Democrats
item.description: Democrats are sounding the alarm over President Trump's move to freeze funding approved in two of former President Biden's signature laws. Trump issued an executive order pausing the disbursement of funds appropriated through the Inflation Reduction Act and the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Alaskans say Trump can change the name of Denali but can't make people call it Mount McKinley
item.description: President Donald Trump has changed the name of Alaska's Denali back to Mount McKinley. It's a nod to President William McKinley, who was from Ohio and who never set foot in Alaska. Many Alaskans say they'll never stop calling the mountain Denali.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump pulls security for Pompeo, others amid Iran threats
item.description: President Trump pulled security protection for former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo and his top aide, Brian Hook, both of whom have faced threats from Iran amid their hard-line positions toward the Islamic Republic, multiple outlets reported on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Early favorite emerges in Republican primary for NJ governor: poll
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – A candidate has emerged as an early favorite in the Republican primary for New Jersey governor, a new Emerson College Polling/PIX11/The Hill poll shows.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11
